name: Brokk
description: Brokkr, often anglicized as Brokk, is a dwarf in Norse mythology, primarily known for his role in the creation of several powerful artifacts for the gods. He is the brother of Eitri (also known as Sindri), and together they are renowned for their unparalleled skill in smithing. Brokkr is portrayed as a diligent and persistent craftsman, willing to endure immense hardships to prove his skill and win wagers. He accepted Loki's challenge to create objects surpassing those crafted by the Sons of Ivaldi, and despite Loki's attempts to sabotage the process, Brokkr and Eitri successfully forged the boar Gullinbursti, the ring Draupnir, and the hammer Mjölnir. Brokkr's determination and craftsmanship are crucial to the Aesir, providing them with weapons and treasures of immense power. His story highlights the importance of craftsmanship and perseverance in Norse cosmology.
subtitle: The Determined Smith

keyMyths.title: The Wager with Loki
keyMyths.description: Loki, ever the trickster, wagered his head that Brokkr and Eitri could not craft items as magnificent as those made by the Sons of Ivaldi. Despite Loki's attempts to sabotage the forge by stinging Brokkr, the brothers persevered. Eitri placed a pigskin in the forge and instructed Brokkr to work the bellows, resulting in the creation of Gullinbursti. He then placed gold in the forge, resulting in Draupnir. Finally, he put iron in the forge, resulting in Mjölnir. Loki lost the wager, though he escaped without losing his head due to a technicality in the agreement concerning his neck.

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Mjölnir
keyMyths.description: The creation of Mjölnir, Thor's hammer, is perhaps Brokkr's most significant accomplishment. While forging the hammer, Loki stung Brokkr on the eyelid, causing him to briefly stop pumping the bellows. This resulted in the hammer having a shorter handle than intended, a flaw that, despite limiting its reach, did not diminish its power. Mjölnir became Thor's signature weapon, instrumental in defending Asgard and Midgard from giants and monsters. The hammer is also used in blessings and sanctifications.

keyMyths.title: Brokkr's Reward
keyMyths.description: After winning the wager with Loki, Brokkr sought to claim his prize: Loki's head. However, Loki argued that the agreement only stipulated his head, and not his neck, which would be injured in the process of removing the head. Brokkr, unable to claim Loki's head directly, instead sewed Loki's mouth shut as punishment for his deceit and trickery. This highlights the Norse emphasis on upholding agreements, even when dealing with dishonest individuals.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson

sources.description: Provides scholarly analysis and interpretation of Norse mythology, including the significance of dwarves and their craftsmanship.
